text: The LORD’s Power Shown in Leviathan

segments.text: Can you pull in Leviathan with a hookor tie down his tongue with a rope?
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Can you put a cord through his noseor pierce his jaw with a hook?
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Will he beg you for mercyor speak to you softly?
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Will he make a covenant with youto take him as a slave for life?
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Can you pet him like a birdor put him on a leash for your maidens?
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Will traders barter for himor divide him among the merchants?
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Can you fill his hide with harpoonsor his head with fishing spears?
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: If you lay a hand on him, you will remember the battle and never repeat it!
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Surely hope of overcoming him is false. Is not the sight of him overwhelming?
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: No one is so fierce as to rouse Leviathan. Then who is able to st and against Me?
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Who has given to Me that I should repay him?[^78] Everything under heaven is Mine.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: I cannot keep silent about his limbs, his power and graceful form.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Who can strip off his outer coat? Who can approach him with a bridle?[^79]
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Who can open his jaws, ringed by his fearsome teeth?
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: His rows of scales are his pride, tightly sealed together.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: One scale is so near to anotherthat no air can pass between them.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: They are joined to one another; they clasp and cannot be separated.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: His snorting flashes with light, and his eyes are like the rays of dawn.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Firebrands stream from his mouth; fiery sparks shoot forth!
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Smoke billows from his nostrilsas from a boiling pot over burning reeds.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: His breath sets coals ablaze, and flames pour from his mouth.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Strength resides in his neck, and dismay leaps before him.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: The folds of his flesh are tightly joined; they are firm and immovable.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: His chest is as hard as a rock, as hard as a lower millstone!
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: When Leviathan rises up, the mighty are terrified; they withdraw before his thrashing.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: The sword that reaches him has no effect, nor does the spear or dart or arrow.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: He regards iron as straw and bronze as rotten wood.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: No arrow can make him flee; slingstones become like chaff to him.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: A club is regarded as straw, and he laughs at the sound of the lance.[^80]
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: His undersides are jagged potsherds, spreading out the mud like a threshing sledge.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: He makes the depths seethe like a cauldron; he makes the sea like a jar of ointment.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: He leaves a glistening wake behind him; one would think the deep had white hair!
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Nothing on earth is his equal—a creature devoid of fear!
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: He looks down on all the haughty; he is king over all the proud.
segments.speaker: The LORD
